author: role.analyst.oceanic-spaces

dimensionSummaries.dimension.player-fantasy: The player is a young sailor-adventurer who turns a vulnerable family rescue into confident navigation across an unknown sea and its scattered communities.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.core-loop: Select a chart heading, set or exploit the wind, sail toward a visible or charted point, investigate an island or platform, gain information or a tool, and return to the sea with more options.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.verbs: Expressive context movement, lock-on swordplay and parries, aimed items, rope traversal, melody performance, sailing, wind control, telescope observation, and limited companion direction form the main vocabulary.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.world-state: Wind direction is directly mutable, chart cells become documented, day and night cycle, and story or item state changes which islands, sea hazards, and submerged rewards can be acted upon.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.topology: A regular ocean grid provides long sightlines and stable headings; distinct islands are separated nodes whose silhouettes, chart cells, and local interiors carry most authored density.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.gating-revisit: Boat access, wind, charts, songs, dungeon tools, story permissions, and late travel abilities gate destinations or actions; earlier islands accumulate new solvable features.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.puzzle-information: Readable animation, gaze, target cues, wind indicators, environmental reactions, and companion constraints expose rules that are tested through tools, timing, direction, or positioning.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.dungeon-structure: Bounded dungeons use keys, central routes, new items, and boss exams; some add an escorted or directed partner whose position becomes part of the dependency graph.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.exploration-reward: Unmarked silhouettes, charts, treasure maps, salvage points, optional islands, platforms, collectibles, and side tasks repeatedly convert visual curiosity into resources or future leads.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.growth-economy: Health, wallets, ammunition capacities, bottles, equipment, and songs persist while rupees, bait, bombs, arrows, and salvage costs regulate preparation and optional discovery.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.combat-boss: Targeted combat adds conspicuous tells and a prompted evasive counter; enemies and bosses emphasize animation-readable openings, spatial reactions, and tool-mediated vulnerability.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.npc-quest: Compact island communities support trading, delivery, photography-like collection, character favors, and reactive dialogue, with facial animation making disposition unusually legible.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.narrative-performance: Sailing departures and arrivals alternate solitude with highly animated encounters; comic reactions, family stakes, and large visual reveals carry drama with relatively little interface interruption.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.camera-blocking: Lock-on, first-person telescope and aiming, wide sea framing, island approach shots, and reaction-focused cuts use silhouette and facial motion to direct attention.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.teaching-feedback: The home island teaches movement, observation, combat, items, and family motivation in a safe circuit; boat guidance, chart markers, target reactions, and expressive animation extend instruction.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.ui-input: The GameCube controller supports analog movement, lock-on, context action, quick item slots, a map and inventory; sailing adds explicit compass, wind, and chart information.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.recovery-accessibility: Saving, fairies, health drops, bottles, dungeon shortcuts, and the boat's stable route network preserve major progress; original-version sailing time can make a mistaken heading costly without being lethal.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.perspective-implementation: Stylized 3D prioritizes silhouette, gaze, squash, and anticipation over texture detail, while ocean scale and horizon visibility make camera distance and streaming central.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.hardware-multiplayer: The main adventure is single-player, with an optional Game Boy Advance plus link-cable role that lets a second participant support from a separate interface without requiring another game copy.
dimensionSummaries.dimension.production-runtime: A faithful abstraction needs streamed sea cells, stable heading and wind simulation, chart discovery, boat transitions, expressive actor animation, target-aware camera, island state, and optional second-device messaging.

observationThemes.context: The player is aboard the boat with a destination or an unfamiliar silhouette visible across open water.
preconditions: The player can consult heading and wind information.
playerInput: Inspect the chart or horizon, choose a heading, set the sail, and correct course during travel.
before: The destination is distant and its local content is unknown.
action: Convert a visual or chart lead into sustained directional movement.
after: The island grows from silhouette to navigable place and a new local interaction loop becomes available.
feedback: Compass heading, wind arrow, boat wake, changing silhouette scale, music transition, and arrival framing show progress.
consequences: Travel itself confirms orientation and seeds optional detours instead of acting only as a loading boundary.
observationThemes.factualDescription: The player sails between islands across a charted sea, using the map, wind direction, horizon, and telescope-like observation to choose or adjust routes.
observationThemes.interpretation: A traversal loop becomes exploratory when distant landmarks continuously turn travel time into prediction and choice.

observationThemes.context: A desired sailing or environmental action depends on direction rather than only possession of an item.
preconditions: A direction-dependent sail or mechanism is present.
playerInput: Perform the control phrase, choose a direction, and resume the dependent action.
before: The current wind makes travel inefficient or leaves the mechanism inactive.
after: Sail response and other wind-sensitive affordances align with the selected direction.
feedback: Wind arrow, cloth and particle motion, sound, boat acceleration, and mechanism response make the state perceptible.
consequences: One explicit world variable connects navigation, puzzle reasoning, and environmental performance.
observationThemes.factualDescription: A learned conducting action lets the player choose wind direction, which directly affects the sailboat and selected environmental interactions.
observationThemes.interpretation: A global rule feels usable when multiple channels render it continuously and its consequences span more than one subsystem.

observationThemes.context: After a long open-water approach, the player reaches an island, sea platform, salvage clue, or bounded dungeon entrance.
preconditions: The node is reachable by boat.
At least one visible or charted affordance invites investigation.
playerInput: Disembark or stop, inspect the compact site, solve its local rule, and collect or record the result.
before: The chart cell or site's opportunity is incomplete or unknown.
action: Trade travel time for a short, dense interaction sequence.
after: The chart, inventory, resource stock, or quest state records a new benefit or lead.
feedback: Distinct island identity, chart update, chest or salvage response, inventory change, and departure view confirm completion.
consequences: Many small nodes make the sparse-looking sea support frequent optional rewards and return goals.
observationThemes.factualDescription: The ocean is divided into chart regions with distinctive islands and smaller encounters; investigation can reveal chart information, treasure, collectibles, resources, quests, or dungeon access.
observationThemes.interpretation: Large traversal spaces can remain legible by concentrating authored interactions into visually singular nodes with explicit completion feedback.

observationThemes.context: A character or enemy notices an event, object, direction, threat, or player action.
preconditions: The actor has a readable reaction state.
Camera distance allows silhouette, gaze, or facial change to be seen.
playerInput: Approach, present an object, trigger the event, or wait for the actor's response.
before: The actor's intent or the next point of attention is uncertain.
action: Cause the actor to enter a reaction animation.
after: Disposition, attention direction, danger, or interaction readiness is clearer.
feedback: Eyes, head turn, pose, timing, sound, and camera emphasis communicate the reaction before or alongside text.
consequences: Performance advances characterization while also reducing dependence on abstract markers.
observationThemes.factualDescription: The stylized characters use pronounced facial and body animation, and the camera frequently gives those reactions space during social, puzzle, and combat moments.
observationThemes.interpretation: One animation system can carry both dramatic subtext and functional guidance when reactions point toward actionable state.

observationThemes.context: A targeted enemy commits to a telegraphed attack that creates a positional counter opportunity.
preconditions: The player is within the valid response window and relation.
playerInput: Read the tell and press the context action during the exposed interval.
before: The enemy controls the exchange or guards ordinary attacks.
after: The hero repositions and the enemy becomes vulnerable or takes damage.
feedback: A brief action prompt, exaggerated anticipation, camera relation, swoosh, and hit reaction distinguish the successful timing.
consequences: The encounter rewards observation and provides a repeatable recovery from offensive pressure.
observationThemes.factualDescription: Targeted combat includes a context-sensitive counter that appears during selected enemy attacks and produces an evasive strike when timed correctly.
observationThemes.interpretation: A narrow prompt can teach timing without replacing observation when it only appears after animation and spatial preconditions are satisfied.

contributionHypotheses: A sparse macro-space can sustain exploration if distant silhouettes continually advertise compact, high-identity interaction nodes.
A player-controlled directional field can unify traversal, puzzle state, visual motion, and ritualized input.
Highly legible actor reactions may carry guidance and characterization through the same authored performance channel.
uniqueOrNonportable: The exact flooded kingdom history, named islands and characters, family rescue plot, conducting instrument, melodies, boat identity, and chart arrangement are recognizable authored surface content and must not be reused.
Specific island silhouettes, dungeon layouts, treasure-chart sequences, enemy counter animations, visual style, and audiovisual motifs are nonportable.
The branded optional handheld-support character and interface are hardware-era particulars; only the abstract asymmetric support relation may be compared.
openQuestions: Original GameCube and HD edition travel-speed, map-interface, and quest changes need explicit delta records before pacing evidence is combined.
Optional link-cable support should receive a separate hardware trace if asymmetric multiplayer patterns are promoted.
